notes: Flat-earth fact-checks exist in the pool but almost exclusively in DE/FR/ES (dpa-Faktencheck, Univision El Detector). The English half of the allowlisted publishers has no earth-shape ClaimReview entries. With same-language retrieval, this case is honestly 'uncovered'. Documents that the EN pool is sparse for niche but well-known conspiracies; cross-lingual matching would close the gap at the cost of NLI reliability (see docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md).
Same coverage gap as 'the earth is round' (EN pool has no earth-shape fact-checks). Pipeline correctly returns uncovered instead of pulling in misrelated topics.
Same coverage gap. Even the direct false claim — which one might expect to find in any English fact-check archive — is not represented in the cleaned EN pool.
Same coverage gap. The polarity-matrix block reflects four phrasings; all fail the same way for the same reason.
direct topical hit, publishers all rated false
strong entailment across multiple publishers and languages

claim: COVID vaccines are safe and effective
notes: true claim contradicted by false anti-vax fact-checks → flip to true

claim: Bill Gates wants to inject everyone with microchips
notes: Pool contains exactly one EN entry from FactCheck.org rated 'False' (`"Bill Gates will use microchip implants to fight coronavirus."`). System correctly reflects that single trustworthy verdict. The pre-cleanup v0 fixture expected 'unknown' because BBC's hedged review was assumed to be in the pool — it isn't anymore (BBC is on the allowlist but their review of this exact framing is not in the Google FCT feed).

claim: Donald Trump won the 2020 US presidential election
notes: Publishers reviewed this exact false claim with verdict false. Confidence settled at ~0.65 in the current pool after cleanup pruned the over-aggregating spam sources that previously inflated it to ~0.94. min_confidence relaxed from 0.7 to 0.6 to reflect post-cleanup reality.

claim: Joe Biden won the 2020 US presidential election
notes: publishers reviewed the negation; contradiction flips false → true
topically uncovered; Stage 3 should mark all candidates neutral
topically near death-hoax claims (Jackie Chan, Mugabe) — NLI must say neutral because the entity differs

German equivalent of #5 (vaccines + microchips). Despite the conspiracy being well-known, the German half of the Google FCT feed has no entries that frame it this way — closest matches are about side-effects, not microchips. Pipeline correctly returns 'uncovered' instead of forcing a verdict from off-topic sources. Documents that the DE pool is thin for some classic conspiracies; English coverage is richer (see #5).

German equivalent of #6 (5G causes COVID). One on-topic dpa-Faktencheck exists in the pool ('Es gibt einen Zusammenhang zwischen 5G und Corona' → debunked) so retrieval + rerank work and one entail vote lands. Verdict is honest 'unknown' because a single vote isn't enough quorum; this test catches that the German path runs end-to-end without crashing, and that the verdict stays cautious when evidence is sparse. English coverage on #6 is richer.
